---
title: "Day 2 - Block 4"
subtitle: "Data Analysis & Coding 1"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D2 B4"
---

## Objective

- Design an orchestrated analysis workflow
- Define tool and memory contracts
- Submit E5 architecture artifact

## Inputs

- One analysis question
- Data schema and constraints
- Tool/memory contract template

## API-first scaffold

```python
# assisted by Cursor AI

def run_analysis_task(data, task_spec):
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4",
        messages=[
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are a reproducible analysis assistant."},
            {"role": "user", "content": f"Task: {task_spec}\nData summary: {data}"}
        ],
        temperature=0.2,
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content
```

## Workflow contract

- Owner per stage
- Input/output schema per handoff
- Escalation target on failure
- Required logging fields

## Test-first acceptance checklist

- Correctness check defined before generation
- One edge case included
- One residual risk recorded
- Re-run steps documented

## Hands-on (E5)

- Architecture sketch + one contract + one fallback path
- Submission: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E5`

## Deliverable

- Workflow architecture diagram
- One stage contract with schema
- One fallback and escalation path

## Timebox

- 20 min lecture input
- 30 min group build
- 10 min debrief

## Evaluation rubric

- Contract clarity at handoffs
- Reproducibility of workflow steps
- Risk and escalation completeness

## Extension task (optional)

- Add one monitoring metric
- Add one rollback rule

## Resource anchors

- R004, R018, R023
- R035, R044/R045

## Debrief

- Which split paid off first?
- What would falsify this architecture?
- Where is mandatory human escalation?
